title: Delhi Health Minister inaugurates advanced 3D dental imaging facility at Maulana Azad Institute
summary: The CBCT system provides high-resolution 3D imaging with faster scan times, typically completed within seconds in a single rotation, while also improving patient comfort by allowing imaging in seated or standing positions.
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
• PTI
  • Published On Apr 29, 2026 at 11:30 PM IST

New Delhi: Delhi Health Minister Pankaj Kumar Singh on Wednesday inaugurated a state-of-the-art 3D dental imaging facility at the Maulana Azad Institute of Dental Sciences, saying the technology will significantly improve diagnostic precision and patient care in the national capital.According to a statement, a Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) machine has been installed at the institute as part of efforts to strengthen advanced healthcare infrastructure in Delhi.

The minister said the CBCT technology offers significantly lower radiation exposure, up to 10 times less than conventional CT scans, along with highly precise diagnostic capabilities, particularly for surgeons and dental specialists.

Advt

He added that the system will enable clinicians to clearly assess critical anatomical structures, including nerves, and plan surgical procedures with greater accuracy.

The CBCT system provides high-resolution 3D imaging with faster scan times, typically completed within seconds in a single rotation, while also improving patient comfort by allowing imaging in seated or standing positions.Singh appreciated the efforts of the institute's medical superintendent and staff for introducing the advanced facility, noting that such initiatives will help improve clinical outcomes and overall patient care.

The facility is also expected to strengthen academic training and research at the institute, allowing students and practitioners to gain hands-on experience with advanced diagnostic tools across specialities such as oral surgery, orthodontics and implantology.The initiative is part of the Delhi government's broader push to enhance healthcare infrastructure and ensure access to modern, patient-centric medical services, the statement added.

  • Published On Apr 29, 2026 at 11:30 PM IST
